seo.title: CPC & Bidding Topic Hub | Ad Breakeven Blog
seo.description: Max CPC formula, break-even bids from max CPA and conversion rate, and practical bidding guidance for Google Ads and Meta campaigns.

headline: CPC & Bidding
intro: Max CPC connects your acquisition cap to click-level bids. If you know max CPA and conversion rate, you know the highest CPC that can still break even — a practical sanity check before raising bids.

sections.heading: From CPA cap to CPC bid

blocks.text: Conversion rate bridges acquisition economics and auction bids. A $50 max CPA at 2% conversion rate means $1.00 break-even CPC. Higher conversion rate raises your affordable CPC; lower rate tightens it.
Max CPC = max CPA × conversion rate (as a decimal)

faq.question: What is max CPC?
faq.answer: Max CPC is the highest cost per click that can still break even given your max CPA and conversion rate.

faq.question: Should I set manual CPC at max CPC?
faq.answer: Max CPC is a ceiling, not a target. Smart bidding may exceed it on some clicks if aggregate CPA stays profitable — but it is a useful planning number.

faq.question: What if conversion rate is zero or unknown?
faq.answer: You cannot calculate break-even CPC without conversion data. Use a test budget, measure conversion rate, then set bids.
